title: Copying subitems between boards with identical items
excerpt: Hello everyone, I have three boards that contain the same items. I updated the subitems on one board and want to copy them to the other two boards.  Some subitems are identical but belong to different parent items.  Is t…

posts.author: Ori11 (Ori)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello everyone, I have three boards that contain the same items. I updated the subitems on one board and want to copy them to the other two boards. Some subitems are identical but belong to different parent items. Is there a simple way to copy or sync s

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ori11,

Welcome to monday community!

I’m not aware of any native monday.com workaround that allows you to sync or copy subitems across multiple boards.

What we use instead is a marketplace app called Same Item Multiple Boards. It creates same items (including subitems) across multiple boards and keeps them in sync, which works well for this kind of setup.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Ori11 Subitems are always tied to their parent item, so there isn’t a simple “copy or sync subitems” option across multiple boards.

The easiest workaround is to duplicate the parent item including its subitems into the other boards.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ori11:

Hello everyone, I have three boards that contain the same items. I updated the subitems on one board and want to copy them to the other two boards. Some subitems are identical but belong to different parent items. Is there a simple way to copy or sync s

Hi @Ori11
You can do this using Make.

Since the boards contain the same items but the subitems belong to different parent items, native features won’t reliably copy or sync subitems across boards — especially when the same subitem names exist under multiple parents. Monday treats each subitem as unique to its parent item, so a direct copy/sync isn’t available.

Here’s how you can handle it:

  • Make watches the board where you updated the subitems.

  • When a subitem is created or updated, Make identifies its parent item.

  • It then finds the matching parent item on the other boards.

  • After that, Make automatically creates or updates the corresponding subitem under the correct parent item on those boards.

This way you’re not just copying text — you’re syncing the structure (parent item → correct subitems), and duplicates are avoided even if subitems have the same names.

If you require additional assistance on setting this automation, please don’t hesitate to reach out to us.
